question: Combine top-k and top-p. Apply top-p first, then top-k if result exceeds k.
code_hint: First apply top-p, then if more than k tokens, keep only top-k
template:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

logits = torch.tensor([1.0, 2.0, 1.0, 4.0])
k = 2
p = 0.9

# Top-p
probs = F.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
sorted_probs, indices = torch.sort(probs, descending=True)
cumsum = torch.cumsum(sorted_probs, dim=-1)
cutoff_idx = torch.where(cumsum >= p)[0][0]
nucleus_size = cutoff_idx.item() + 1

# Apply top-k
final_k = min(???)
print(f"Combined top-k={k}, top-p={p}: keep {final_k} tokens")
answer:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

logits = torch.tensor([1.0, 2.0, 1.0, 4.0])
k = 2
p = 0.9

# Top-p
probs = F.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
sorted_probs, indices = torch.sort(probs, descending=True)
cumsum = torch.cumsum(sorted_probs, dim=-1)
cutoff_idx = torch.where(cumsum >= p)[0][0]
nucleus_size = cutoff_idx.item() + 1

# Apply top-k
final_k = min(nucleus_size, k)
print(f"Combined top-k={k}, top-p={p}: keep {final_k} tokens")
